title: chrysophyllum durifructum
intro: Chrysophyllum durifructum, also known as the White Ironwood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. The bark, leaves, and fruit of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. Chrysophyllum durifructum is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and are used to treat a range of ailments, including wounds, infections, and digestive issues.
